heading: How equal are women and men in the EU?
subheading: The EU Gender Equality Index for 2025 stands at 63.4 out of 100, reflecting uneven progress among member states. Over the past five years, Malta (+8.6), Ireland (+7.8) and Lithuania (+6.9) have made the most significant progress, while the EU as a whole has seen an increase of +3.5 points in the index. In contrast, countries such as Bulgaria (-0.7), Croatia (+0.3), Latvia (+0.4) and Hungary (+0.5) have made little or no progress.
textNote: The Gender Equality Index is a tool to measure the progress of gender equality in the EU. It examines how elements such as disability, age, level of education, country of birth and family type can intersect with gender.
